title: facility — subprocessors
description: The Facility module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Stackra platform. The Notifications module dispatches booking confirmations + reminders via its own subprocessors (mail, SMS, push); those are declared in notifications::subprocessors.json. The Finance module (Wave 4) will engage payment processors for the money side of bookings + day-passes + passes; those live in finance::subprocessors.json. This file lists ZERO active subprocessors to be explicit about the module's boundary — booking state is fully first-party.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Dispatching BookingConfirmed / BookingCancelled / BookingReminder / FacilityBlackoutAffectsBooking / PassIssued / PassExhausted / PassExpiring / PassRevoked notifications via mail / SMS / push channels.
Payment processing for booking prices + day-pass sales + membership renewals that provision passes. Stripe / Paddle / Adyen depending on region.

notes: This file exists deliberately even with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

If a future Wave introduces a subprocessor into this module (e.g. an availability-optimisation AI service), add it here + update compliance.json Art. 28 evidence + refresh the tenant-facing subprocessor list.
